parsed_review.summary: CP00-202 (RP06 DMS Core primary/secondary workflow descriptor pack, Risk B, 40 units RP06.P02.M03.S01-RP06.P02.M04.S15) passes read-only review with one non-blocking P3 finding. The pack is descriptor-only and every required boundary is implemented and asserted: persistence_boundary and state_transition_enforcement set writes_product_state/creates_database_rows/persists_workflow_attempt/persists_idempotency_key/acquires_runtime_lock/runtime_state_write all false, and validateDmsCoreCp202WorkflowDescriptor enforces these plus executes_runtime:false and the full no-write attestation (dispatches_primary/secondary/review/approval_workflow_runtime all false). Authority boundary is intact: the validator rejects promotes_claude_to_final_approval and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation unless both false, the Claude review packet is read_only with required_model claude-opus-4-8, and Hermes patterns are confined to the H06 deterministic evidence/receipt packet (receipt_shape descriptor_only...no_write), not product runtime. CP178+ hardening is present: forbidden_review_evidence/invalid_review_blockers enumerate auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed JSON, fenced-JSON-plus-prose, tool-call-shaped output, claude-final-approval, source-mutation, and production/enterprise-trust claims. All 9 required public exports exist across service.js/validators.js and are listed in registry.js; index.js re-exports them; README documents the CP00-202 section; tests in model.test.js cover the 40-unit distribution, descriptor/route binding without runtime dispatch, and authority-boundary flags. command-evidence.json records all 17 commands at exit_code 0 (including npm test, npm run validate, build, spec:requirements/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane validate, rp06:dms-core:validate, closeout-pack:validate -- CP00-202, and git diff --check) with production_ready:false, no_real_data:true. The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly NOT asserted: claude-review-result.json (pending), adjudication.md (pending), and construction-inspection.json (PENDING, production_ready:false) all reflect the expected pre-review state where this C06 review is the gate; nothing overclaims production readiness or enterprise trust, and human final approval remains required downstream. No P0 or P1 issues found. The single P3 finding notes that the expected command npm run rp05:matter-core:validate is not recorded in command-evidence.json and, unlike the explicitly-deferred closeout-pack-plan:validate, is unexplained; impact is low because this is an RP06 (not RP05) pack and matter-core tests run transitively under the passing npm test.

findings.title: Expected validation command rp05:matter-core:validate is not recorded or explained in command-evidence.json
findings.details: The review prompt's expected pre-closeout validation commands include `npm run rp05:matter-core:validate`. command-evidence.json records the program-relevant `npm run rp06:dms-core:validate` plus the other expected commands, but omits `npm run rp05:matter-core:validate`. The omission of `npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate` is explicitly explained in the notes array (deferred until after production_ready and queue regeneration), whereas the rp05 omission has no accompanying note. Impact is low: CP00-202 is an RP06 (DMS Core) pack, not an RP05 (Matter Core) pack; npm test exits 0 and transitively exercises the matter-core test suite; and the RP06-specific contract validation (rp06:dms-core:validate) is present and passing. No no-write, no-real-data, authority-boundary, or Hermes-scope guarantee is affected.
findings.recommendation: Before final human approval, either record the `npm run rp05:matter-core:validate` exit status in command-evidence.json, or add a note (mirroring the closeout-pack-plan:validate explanation) clarifying that rp05 matter-core validation is out of direct scope for this RP06 pack and is covered transitively by npm test.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Does not block pack or goal closeout because the pack is RP06 (not RP05) and matter-core coverage is transitive via the passing npm test; recorded for evidence-completeness and to be resolved by the pack author with a one-line note or the explicit command entry.
